response: Creating test variants allows you to personalize the evaluation based on roles, skills, or other criteria. This approach eliminates the need to manage separate tests, improves efficiency, and ensures candidates are assessed on the most relevant content. To create test variants, log in to your HackerRank for Work account, go to the Tests tab, open the test you want to modify, go to the Questions tab, click the more option menu (⋮) in the top right corner, and select Create Variants. The system converts the existing test into Variant 1 and opens Variant 2, where you can start creating the second version. To add more variants, select the + icon next to the tabs. Note: A variant-based test requires at least two variants.
justification: The support ticket asks about best practices for when to create a new test versus create a variant of the test. The provided support documentation includes a section on 'Create Test Variants' which explains how to create test variants and their benefits. [Source: data/hackerrank/screen/managing-tests/7530103378-test-variants.md] Ticket contains multiple requests; primary issue addressed, secondary requests may require follow-up.

response: To add extra time for a candidate, log in to your HackerRank for Work account and go to the Tests tab. Select the test you want to modify and go to the Candidates tab. Select the checkbox next to the candidate(s) and click More > Add Time. Enter the time in minutes and click Confirm. Please note that a candidate who is still in a test session must refresh the page to see the updated timer. A candidate who has completed the test must log in again to view the added time.
justification: The support ticket is about adding extra time for a candidate in a Hackerrank assessment. The solution is provided in the documentation 'data/hackerrank/screen/managing-tests/4811403281-adding-extra-time-for-candidates.md'. response: To delete your HackerRank Community account, you must first set a password if you signed up using a third-party account such as Google. Please follow the steps in the 'Resetting password' documentation to set a password. Once you have set a password, you can delete your account by logging in to the HackerRank Community, clicking the profile icon, selecting 'Settings', scrolling to the 'Delete Accounts' section, and following the prompts. If you accidentally delete your account, contact help@hackerrank.com to recover it.
justification: The user wants to delete their account, which falls under the 'Account Access' product area. The documentation states that a password must be set before deleting an account if the user signed up using a third-party account. The response provides instructions for setting a password and deleting the account, as well as information on how to recover an accidentally deleted account.
